one_time_description: Historic land claim settlement agreement with the Government of Canada for $310,000,000; $77,955,000 distributed to adult members; $228,207,067 deferred to subsequent year; net impact $3,837,933 recognized in current year
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Gambler Management Limited Partnership and Gambler Development Limited Partnership (equity method). Related party entities include Gambler Resources LP, Gambler Gaming Ventures LP, Gambler Construction LP, Gambler Brandon Ventures LP, Gambler Gas & Convenience Store LP, Falcon Management LP, Mackinley-Clark Paving Ltd., Odanah Truck Line Inc. Multiple construction projects underway including CMHC Phase 8 and 9 housing, Cultural Centre, duplex, Brandon Tipi project. $310M land claim settlement is transformative economic event. John/James Tanner Gambler Legacy Trust established with net assets of $204,933,958.
notes: Major one-time event: $310M land claim settlement with Canada recognized in fiscal 2024. After $77.955M member distributions and $228.207M deferred to subsequent year, net $3.837M recognized. John/James Tanner Gambler Legacy Trust established with $204.9M in assets. $17.05M held in trust related to disputed legal/consulting fees from settlement proceeds. Deferred revenue of $233.85M on balance sheet ($5.65M program + $228.2M land claim). Province is Manitoba not AB. Auditor is Lazer Grant LLP (Winnipeg). No schedule of remuneration for chief/council provided. Segment disclosure shows Claims segment with $81.1M expenses and $4.7M surplus. Two GBEs accounted for by equity method: Gambler Management LP and Gambler Development LP. Several related-party LPs not consolidated. osr calculated as total revenue $11,432,967 minus ISC $7,079,911 minus CMHC $258,955 = $4,094,101.
